title: Symphyotrichum × patulum
taxonomy: Symphyotrichum × patulum is a hybrid of the Aster family. It is classified as a member of the family Asteraceae. This plant is further categorized as a member of the genus Symphyotrichum. Within the genus, Symphyotrichum × patulum is often referred to as a distinct species. As a result, its classification is Symphyotrichum × patulum (Asteraceae: Asteraceae, Symphyotrichum).
uses: Symphyotrichum × patulum is commonly known as the New England aster, and its medicinal uses are not well-documented. However, its plant family, Asteraceae, has several members that have been used in traditional medicine to treat various health issues. More research is needed to fully understand the medicinal potential of this plant.

intro: Symphyotrichum × patulum, also known as broad-leaved aster, is a hybrid plant species within the Asteraceae family, characterized by its broad leaves and showy purple flowers. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can be attributed to its bioactive constituents, such as flavonoids, phenolic acids, and sesquiterpene lactones. These bioactive compounds have been shown to possess antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antiviral activities, making Symphyotrichum × patulum a promising natural remedy for various ailments. The plant is commonly used in herbal medicine and is often prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, with the dried flowers and leaves being the most commonly used parts for herbal preparations.
